questions.question: Here, a problem or situation is given in the statement you have to choose the appropriate logical deduction for given statement. Statement: Wildlife safaris generate funds that support conservation activities. However, at certain reserves, tourists have been seen stepping out of vehicles, feeding animals, and crowding sightings, which disrupts natural behaviour. Park management is now limiting vehicles per zone and issuing fines for violations to protect wildlife. Conclusions: I. Wildlife safaris should be stopped everywhere without exception. II. Some tourist behaviour can disturb wildlife and its natural patterns. III. Park management is taking steps to reduce harm caused during safaris. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
options: Only conclusions I and III follow.
Only conclusions I and II follow.
Only conclusions II and III follow.
questions.explanation: Conclusion I does not follow because the statement highlights issues in certain reserves and talks about regulation, not total shutdown everywhere. Conclusion II follows because the statement explicitly mentions actions that disrupt animal behaviour. Conclusion III follows because limiting vehicles and fining violations are clear steps to reduce harm. Hence, only II and III follow

questions.question: In each question below is given a statement followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which of the suggested courses of action logically follow(s) for pursuing. Statement: Farmers in a district are not receiving crop-insurance payouts because many claim records contain small bank-detail mismatches, causing repeated payment failures despite valid claims. Course of action I: Discontinue the crop-insurance scheme permanently since payouts are getting delayed. Course of action II: Advise farmers to avoid sowing the next season until the payment mismatch issue is fully resolved.
options: Only course of action II follows.
Both courses of action I and II follow.
Only course of action I follows.
Neither course of action I nor II follows.
questions.explanation: Course of action I is not logical because a correctable operational error is not a valid reason to end the entire scheme. Course of action II is also not logical because stopping sowing harms livelihoods and food supply; the issue needs administrative correction, not farming shutdown. Therefore, neither course of action I nor II follows.

questions.question: In this type of questions, question is provided with some issues/disputes/problem, followed by few methods to resolve the problem in statement choosing the appropriate problem solving method that can be implemented. A training institute conducts computer-based mock tests. Invigilators notice some candidates wearing smartwatches and using quick glance signals during questions. The institute wants a preventive measure that reduces cheating in future tests without creating a harsh environment for honest candidates. What is the best preventive measure?

Cancel all tests for the batch
questions.explanation: Smartwatches enable hidden communication and quick reference, so they must be removed before the test. Randomized question order reduces the benefit of signaling even if someone attempts it. This prevents cheating proactively while keeping the test process fair.

questions.question: 26 students are standing in a line. B is standing in 7ᵗʰ place from the left of the line. C is standing in the 10ᵗʰ place after B. D is standing at the 12ᵗʰ place from the right of the line. How many students are standing between C and D?
questions.explanation: Total students = 26 B is in 7ᵗʰ place from the left and C is 10ᵗʰ place after B. Which means C will be in 17ᵗʰ place from the left. D is at the 12ᵗʰ place from the right which means D is at 15ᵗʰ place from the left. Total students between C and D =(17 -15)-1=2-1=1 So, one student stand between C and D.

questions.question: Kirti said to her mother, "My friend Ayesha left her hometown today and will reach our home tomorrow. Her job interview is day after tomorrow, which is Friday. She had applied for the job day before yesterday." On which day did Ayesha apply for the job?

questions.question: Bimbisara was the ruler of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara was one of the early and most prominent rulers of the Haryanka dynasty, which ruled in ancient Magadha (modern-day Bihar) around the 6th century BCE. King Bimbisara was killed by his son, Prince Ajatashatru. King Pasenadi, king of neighbouring Kosala and brother-in-law of King Bimbisara, promptly retook the gift of the Kashi province. Further insights: Satavahana dynasty - An ancient Indian dynasty of the Deccan; Gautamiputra Satakarni was one of its most prominent rulers. Pallava dynasty - A South Indian dynasty; Narasimhavarman I (also known as Mamalla) was a famous ruler noted for building Mahabalipuram. Khilji dynasty - A dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate; Alauddin Khilji was its most powerful ruler, known for his military conquests and economic reforms.

questions.question: Select the correct statement(s) of the following with respect to judges of Supreme Courts and High Courts: A. In order to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court, a person must be a citizen of India and must have been a Judge of a High Court or of two or more such Courts in succession for at least five years. B. A judge of the High Court can work till the age of 65 years.
questions.explanation: In order to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court, a person must be a citizen of India and must have been - • For atleast five years, a Judge of a High Court or of two or more such Courts in succession, or • An Advocate of a High Court or of two or more such Courts in succession for at least 10 years, or • He must be, in the opinion of the President, a distinguished jurist. A judge of the Supreme Court can work till the age of 65. A Judge of the Supreme Court cannot be removed from office except by an order of the President passed after an address in each House of Parliament supported by a majority of the total membership of that House and by a majority of not less than two-thirds of members present and voting. Retirement age for judges of High Courts is 62 years.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true? a. Kumudini Lakhia was recently conferred with the Padma Vibhushan on 2025 Republic Day in recognition of her lifelong dedication to Kuchipudi. b. She had earlier been awarded the Padma Shri in 1987 and the Padma Bhushan in 2010. c. She established the Kadamb Centre for Dance in Ahmedabad.
questions.explanation: Eminent Kathak exponent and choreographer Kumudini Lakhia passed away at her residence in Ahmedabad on April 12, 2025 at the age of 95 due to an age-related ailment. Lakhia was recently conferred with the Padma Vibhushan on 2025 Republic Day in recognition of her lifelong dedication to Kathak. She had earlier been awarded the Padma Shri in 1987 and the Padma Bhushan in 2010, along with several other prestigious honours throughout her illustrious career. Lakhia was born in 1929. She began her career dancing with Ram Gopal and toured internationally, bringing Indian classical dance forms to global audiences. She later trained under maestros of the Jaipur Gharana and Pandit Shambhu Maharaj. Over time, she evolved from a solo performer into a pioneering choreographer. After more than two decades of solo performances, in 1967, Lakhia established the Kadamb Centre for Dance in Ahmedabad.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement. I. The Sixth Five-Year Plan marked the beginning of economic liberalisation. II. The Minimum Needs Programme (MNP) was introduced in the first year of the Sixth Five-Year Plan.
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct, while Statement II is incorrect. Explanation of Statements: Statement I is correct. The Sixth Five-Year Plan (19801985) is considered to have marked the beginning of economic liberalization in India, albeit in a limited form. The plan moved away from the Nehruvian socialist model towards a more pragmatic approach. It saw the end of price controls, the rationalization of the tax system, and a greater emphasis on infrastructure and the private sector, setting the stage for the more comprehensive reforms of 1991. The slogan "Garibi Hatao" was also operationalized during this plan. Statement II is incorrect. The Minimum Needs Programme (MNP), which aimed to provide a minimum level of social and economic infrastructure to the poor, was introduced in the first year of the Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-1978), not the Sixth. It was launched in 1974 with the objective of "direct attack on poverty." Further insights: The Sixth Five-Year Plan was a significant success, with the Indian economy growing at a rate of 5.7% against a target of 5.2%. The plan's focus was on increasing national income, modernizing technology, ensuring a continuous decrease in poverty and unemployment, and strengthening the industrial and energy base. The National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) was established during this plan in 1982.

questions.question: Which phylum is characterized by the presence of nematocysts and radial symmetry?
questions.explanation: Cnidarians (phylum Cnidaria) are characterized by: · Nematocysts: Specialized stinging cells used for defense and capturing prey. · Radial symmetry: Their body parts are arranged symmetrically around a central axis. Further insights: · Platyhelminthes (Flatworms) - Bilateral symmetry, no nematocysts. · Annelida (Segmented worms) - Bilateral symmetry, no nematocysts. · Arthropoda (Insects, Crustaceans, etc.) - Bilateral symmetry, no nematocysts.

questions.question: Which among the following statements about workforce in India is true?
options: The workforce in secondary sector is more than tertiary sector.
The number of female workers in high in tertiary sector than other two sectors.
The work participation rate is higher in areas of lower economic development.
The proportion of workers is more than non-workers in India.
questions.explanation: Option 'a' is incorrect: Tertiary sector employees 41.6% of total workers, Primary sector employees 54.6% and secondary sector employees 3.8%. Option 'b' is incorrect: The number of female workers is relatively high in primary sector, though in recent years there has been some improvement in work participation of women in secondary and tertiary sectors. Option 'c' is correct: Work Participation rate measures active labor force and is the sum of all employed workers divided by the working age population. It refers to the number of people who are either employed or are actively looking for work. In Indian context the work participation rate tends to be higher in the areas of lower levels of economic development since large number of manual workers are needed to perform the subsistence or near subsistence economic activities. Option 'd' is incorrect: In India, the proportion of workers (both main and marginal) is only 39.8 per cent (2011) leaving a vast majority of about 60 per cent as non-workers. Source: India People and Economy NCERT

questions.question: Which of the following is a genre of semi-classical singing, popular in Uttar Pradesh and Bihar and is also called Dhamar?
questions.explanation: Hori is a genre of semi-classical (or light classical) Indian music traditionally sung during the spring festival of Holi. It is particularly popular in Uttar Pradesh and Bihar. Hori compositions often describe the playful interactions between Radha and Krishna during Holi, or the general revelry of the festival. One form of Hori singing is specifically associated with the Dhamar taal (a 14-beat rhythmic cycle) and is hence sometimes referred to as Hori-Dhamar or simply Dhamar when sung in this style within the classical Dhrupad tradition (though Hori itself also exists as a lighter, folk-influenced form). Explanation of Other Options: (a) Bhajan: Devotional songs, often with simple melodies and religious themes, popular across India in various languages and traditions. (b) Ghazal: A poetic form consisting of rhyming couplets and a refrain, with each line sharing the same meter. As a musical genre, it is a light classical form of sung poetry, popular in North India and Pakistan. (d) Qawwali: A form of Sufi Islamic devotional singing, originating from the Indian subcontinent, known for its powerful vocals, rhythmic hand-clapping, and often ecstatic performances. Further insights: Hori songs are characterized by their festive and playful mood, often depicting scenes of throwing colors, teasing, and romance associated with the Holi festival. In the classical tradition, Hori-Dhamar is a demanding vocal form that requires mastery over rhythm and improvisation within the Dhamar taal. Besides its classical and semi-classical forms, Hori also exists as a vibrant folk music tradition in regions like Braj (Uttar Pradesh).

questions.question: With reference to the Government of India Act, 1935, consider the following statements: a. It introduced provincial autonomy and diarchy was abolished at the provincial level. b. It provided for the establishment of an All-India Federation. c. It extended the right to vote to all adult citizens of India. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 'a' and 'b' are correct, while statement 'c' is incorrect. Explanation of Statements: Statement a is correct: A key feature of the Government of India Act, 1935, was the introduction of provincial autonomy. It abolished the system of diarchy (dual government with 'reserved' and 'transferred' subjects) at the provincial level, which had been established by the Act of 1919, and made provincial governments responsible to their own legislatures. Statement b is correct: The Act provided for the establishment of an All-India Federation consisting of British Indian provinces and the princely states. However, this provision never came into effect because the required number of princely states did not agree to join the federation. Statement c is incorrect: The Act extended the franchise, but it did not introduce universal adult suffrage. The right to vote was still restricted based on qualifications like property ownership, tax payment, and education. Only about 10-14% of the population of British India was given the right to vote. Universal adult suffrage was introduced later by the Constitution of independent India. Further insights: While the Act abolished diarchy at the provincial level, it introduced it at the Centre (though this part was also not implemented). The Act provided for the establishment of a Federal Court (which later became the Supreme Court of India) and the Reserve Bank of India. Many provisions of the Government of India Act, 1935, particularly those related to the federal structure and administrative details, were incorporated into the Constitution of India.

questions.question: With reference to NISAR, consider the following statements: i. It is an SUV-sized satellite that is being jointly developed by the space agencies of Russia and India. ii. it is the satellite that consists of both L-band and S-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR) instruments, which makes it a dual-frequency imaging radar satellite. iii. It has a large 39-foot stationary antenna reflector made of a gold-plated wire mesh. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Jointly developed by NASA and the ISRO, an Earth-observation satellite, called NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar), got a send-off ceremony at the American space agency's Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL). Statement i is not correct:It's an SUV-sized satellite that is being jointly developed by the space agencies of the US and India. • The partnership agreement was signed between NASA and ISRO in September 2014, according to which NASA will provide one of the radars for the satellite, a high-rate communication subsystem for science data, GPS receivers, and a payload data subsystem. • The satellite will be launched in 2022 from the Satish Dhawan Space Center in Sriharikota, India, into a near-polar orbit and will scan the globe every 12 days over the course of its three-year mission of imaging the Earth's land, ice sheets, and sea ice to give an "unprecedented" view of the planet. • ISRO, on the other hand, will provide the spacecraft bus, the second type of radar (called the S-band radar), the launch vehicle, and associated launch services. • The goal of NISAR is to make global measurements of the causes and consequences of land surface changes using advanced radar imaging. Statement ii is correct:The satellite consists of both L-band and S-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR) instruments,which makes it a dual-frequency imaging radar satellite. • While NASA has provided the L-band radar, GPS, a high-capacity solid-state recorder to store data, and a payload data subsystem, ISRO has provided the S-band radar, the GSLV launch system, and spacecraft. Statement iii is correct:It has a large 39-foot stationary antenna reflector made of a gold-plated wire mesh, the reflector will be used to focus "the radar signals emitted and received by the upward-facing feed on the instrument structure".

questions.question: Select the option that is true regarding the following two statements labelled Assertion(A) and Reason(R). Assertion (A): Officers of the All India Services (AIS) are subject to "unitary control," where the State Government exercises immediate control and the Central Government exercises ultimate control. Reason (R): While a State Government can suspend an IAS officer, only the Central Government has the legal authority to dismiss or remove them from service.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Assertion (A): Unitary Control over AIS (True) The All India Services (AIS), which include the IAS, IPS, and IFoS, are unique because they are recruited and trained by the Central Government but assigned to various State Government cadres. · Dual Control: They work under a system of dual control. The State Government exercises "immediate control" because the officer is serving under its administrative jurisdiction (handling day-to-day tasks, postings, and transfers within the state). · Ultimate Control: The Central Government exercises "ultimate control" as the appointing authority. This ensures that the services maintain an "all-India character" and are not entirely subservient to local political pressures. Reason (R): Powers of Suspension vs. Dismissal (True) The disciplinary powers are distributed to maintain this balance: · Suspension: A State Government has the authority to suspend an AIS officer serving under its affairs if disciplinary proceedings are contemplated or pending. · Dismissal/Removal: According to Article 311 of the Constitution and the All India Services (Discipline and Appeal) Rules, 1969, no member of an AIS can be dismissed or removed by an authority subordinate to that by which they were appointed. Since they are appointed by the President of India (Central Government), only the Central Government has the legal power to impose "major penalties" like dismissal, removal, or compulsory retirement. Why R explains A The fact that the State can only take temporary actions (like suspension) while the final, life-altering professional decisions (like dismissal) rest solely with the Centre is the direct functional manifestation of the "unitary control" mentioned in the assertion. It proves that while the State manages the officer immediately, the ultimate leash is held by the Union. Further insights: · Article 312 empowers Parliament to create All India Services common to the Union and the States in the national interest. · Creation of a new AIS requires a Rajya Sabha resolution passed by a two-thirds majority of members present and voting. · Article 312 acts as the constitutional foundation for "dual control" over AIS officers by the Centre and States.

questions.question: The monsoon is experienced in the tropical area between 20⁰ N and 20⁰ S. Which of the following is/are the factors that influences the formation of Indian monsoon in this region? I. Shift in the ITCZ position II. Presence of high pressure near Madagascar region. III. Formation of high pressure over the Tibetan plateau Select the answer using the codes given below.
questions.explanation: Statement I and II are correct: Inter Tropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ): It is broad trough of low pressure in equatorial latitudes. This is where the northeast and the southeast trade winds converge. This convergence zone lies more or less parallel to the equator but moves north or south with the apparent movement of the sun. The shift of position of ITCZ occurs in summer over the Ganga plain. This is the equatorial trough normally positioned about 5 degree N of the equator. It is also known as the monsoon- trough during the monsoon season. Presence of high-pressure area east of Madagascar: It is approximately found at 20degree S over the Indian Ocean. The intensity and position of this high-pressure area affects the Indian Monsoon. Statement III is incorrect: Heating of Tibetan plateau: the Tibetan plateau gets intensely heated during summer, which results in strong vertical air currents and the formation of low pressure over the plateau at about 9km above the sea level. The differential heating and cooling of land and water: This creates low pressure on the landmass of India while the seas around experience comparatively high pressure. Source: 9ᵗʰ std.-NCERT

questions.question: With reference to the Preamble of Indian Constitution, which of the statements is/are correct? 1) The preamble has been amended only once so far. 2) In the Kesavananda Bharati case, Supreme Court held that the Preamble is not a part of constitution. Select the correct answers from the codes given below.
questions.explanation: The question as to whether the Preamble can be amended under Article 368 of the Constitution arose for the first time in the historic case of Kesavananda Bharati (1973). It was urged that the Preamble cannot be amended as it is not a part of the Constitution. The Supreme Court, however, held that the Preamble is a part of the Constitution. The Court stated that the opinion tendered by it in the Berubari Union (1960) in this regard was wrong, and held that the Preamble can be amended, subject to the condition that no amendment is done to the 'basic features'. The Preamble has been amended only once so far, in 1976, by the 42ⁿᵈ Constitutional Amendment Act, which has added three new words - Socialist, Secular and Integrity - to the Preamble.

questions.question: Which of the following reforms is/are done by Alauddin Khilji? i) He implemented a strict system of market control to regulate prices and prevent hoarding ii) He introduced a new land revenue system known as kharja iii) He introduces Chehra and Dagh system for identification of Soldier and Horses
questions.explanation: • Alauddin Khilji (1296-1316) o Killed his father-in-law Jalaluddin Khilji and succeeded the throne o He won Gujarat (1298), Ranthambhor (1301), ▪ Alauddin Army led by Malik Kafur defeated • Yadavs of Devagiri • Kakatiya's of Warangal • Hoysalas of Dwarsamudra • Pandya of Madurai o Reforms by Khilji ▪ Issued 4 ordinance to control the noble of the Empire ▪ Introduce the system of Dagh (branding of horse) and Chehra (descriptive identity of soldier) ▪ Market reform to control the market. ▪ Introduce new land revenue system known as kharja in which all the land was to be measured and state share was to be fixed its about 50% of the peasant production. • Form special officer called Mustakharaj for collection of revenue.

questions.question: Three friends deposit amounts in the ratio 2:3:5 at 12% p.a. simple interest for 4 years. If the total interest earned is Rs 9600, find the smallest deposit.
questions.explanation: Let deposits be 2k, 3k, 5k Interest factor for 4 years at 12% = 48% = 0.48 Total interest = 0.48 X (2k + 3k + 5k) = 0.48 X 10k = 4.8k Now, 4.8k = 9600 => k = 2000 So, smallest deposit = 2k = 4000

questions.question: The respective ratio between the present age of Anu and Rahul is 5 : a. Anu is 9 years younger than Dinesh. Dinesh age after 9 years will be 33 years. The difference between Rahul and Anu’s age is same as the present age of Dinesh. What is the value of a ?

questions.question: A rectangular park has paths inside it along all its edges. The width of the path is 2 m while the length and breadth of the park is 15 m and 10 m respectively. 28 m² of the path is covered with slabs of stones while the remaining path is covered with artificial grass. questions.question: Pooja and Raghav invested Rs. 50,000 and Rs. 40,000 respectively for the whole year. Pooja is entitled to a salary of Rs. 2,000 per month from the year's profit, taken before division. If the total profit is Rs. 1,32,000, how much does Raghav receive?

questions.question: A shopkeeper sold three electronic gadgets, a headphone, a speaker, and a smartwatch, and their selling prices were in the ratio of 3: 4: 5 respectively. He suffered a 20% loss on the headphone, a 25% profit on the speaker and no profit and no loss on the smartwatch. What was his overall percentage gain or loss in the entire transaction?

questions.question: The selling price of article A is Rs. 3240. The cost price of article B is 20% more than the cost price of article A. If the profit percentages on articles A and B are 35% and 25% respectively, then find the selling price of article B.
questions.explanation: Let the cost price of article A be Rs. 100x. questions.question: An article costs Rs. 18500 without GST. Initially, the GST rate on the article was 28%, but was later reduced to 12%. By how much percentage was the price of the article with GST reduced after the reduction in rates?

questions.question: A solid brass sphere of radius 6 cm is melted. During recasting, one-ninth of the metal is lost. The remaining metal is used to form smaller spheres, each of radius 2 cm. How many smaller spheres can be made?
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere = (4/3) X π X r³ Original volume of the brass sphere = (4/3) X π X 6³ One-ninth of the metal is lost Remaining metal = (8/9) of the original volume Let the number of smaller spheres be n n X (4/3) X π X 2³ = (8/9) X (4/3) X π X 6³ n X 8 = (8/9) X 216 n X 8 = 192 n = 24

questions.question: According to the passage, what assumption do companies often make while using AI systems?
options: AI systems can completely replace human supervision in complex tasks.
The correct input will always produce flawless output.
Automation reduces the need for technological infrastructure.
AI systems function independently of existing data patterns.
questions.explanation: The passage states, "there is often an implicit assumption that once the right input is given, the output will be flawless." (Paragraph 1). This clearly shows that companies assume perfect input will lead to perfect output. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: According to the passage, what is a consequence of over-generalising content through automation?
options: It leads to highly personalised and diverse outputs.
It results in uniform content lacking originality and depth.
It improves the creative capabilities of artificial intelligence.
It eliminates the need for human intervention in content creation.
questions.explanation: The passage explains, "the output begins to converge into a uniform style.. such mass-produced material risks becoming a 'heap of sameness,' where quality is sacrificed for quantity." (Paragraph 2). This indicates that over-generalisation leads to lack of originality and depth. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best represents the main idea of the passage?
options: Automation completely eliminates the role of human workers in modern industries.
Automation is beneficial but still requires human oversight and creativity.
Artificial intelligence is incapable of performing repetitive tasks efficiently.
Companies should avoid using AI systems in content creation processes.
questions.explanation: The passage highlights both the benefits and limitations of automation and concludes that human involvement remains essential. It states, "automation has merely shifted their role - from execution to supervision and validation" (Paragraph 2) and further adds, "the most effective approach lies in combining technological efficiency with human insight." (Paragraph 3). This shows that automation must be complemented by human creativity and oversight. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The word "nuanced" in the passage most nearly means:
options: Completely irrelevant or unnecessary in context.
Broad and general without specific details.
Simple and easily understood without effort.
Subtle and involving fine distinctions in meaning.
questions.explanation: The passage states, "it lacks the nuanced understanding, contextual sensitivity, and critical judgement that human intelligence brings." (Paragraph 2). Here, "nuanced" refers to the ability to understand subtle differences and complexities. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Identify the type of conjunction used in the word "while" in the sentence: "While automation can handle repetitive and structured tasks remarkably well, it lacks the nuanced understanding.."
options: Correlative conjunction used in paired structures.
Subordinating conjunction introducing a dependent clause.
questions.explanation: In the sentence, "While automation can handle repetitive and structured tasks remarkably well.." (Paragraph 2), the word "while" introduces a dependent clause that contrasts with the main clause. A conjunction that introduces a dependent clause is called a subordinating conjunction. Coordinating conjunction (joining equal clauses) · Examples: and, but, or, nor, for, so, yet · Joins two independent clauses of equal importance. Correlative conjunction (used in pairs) · Examples: either..or, neither..nor, both..and Subordinating conjunction (introducing a dependent clause) · Examples: because, although, since, if, when, while Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct one-word substitute for: 'Eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant.'
questions.explanation: The correct one-word substitute for 'Eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant' is (a) Truculent. (a) Truculent: This is the correct answer. It describes someone who is eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant. A truculent person is often bad-tempered and ready for a confrontation. (b) Amicable: This refers to something (especially a relationship or agreement) characterized by friendliness and absence of discord. An amicable divorce is one where both parties remain friendly. (c) Compliant: This describes someone who is disposed to agree with others or obey rules, especially to an excessive degree; acquiescent. A compliant employee follows instructions readily. (d) Phlegmatic: This describes a person having an unemotional and stolidly calm disposition. A phlegmatic individual is not easily excited or upset and tends to remain composed in stressful situations.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The historians were meticulously examining ancient manuscripts to piece together the history of the forgotten kingdom from the medieval era.
options: Ancient manuscripts had been meticulously examined by the historians to piece together the history of the forgotten kingdom from medieval era.
Ancient manuscripts are being meticulously examined by the historians to piece together the history of the forgotten kingdom from the medieval era.
Ancient manuscripts were meticulously examined by the historians to piece together the history of the forgotten kingdom from the medieval era.
Ancient manuscripts were being meticulously examined by the historians to piece together the history of the forgotten kingdom from the medieval era.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a "to be" form of verb ['being' for the given sentence] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. In Passive Voice, the preposition "by" is used before the object. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in the Past Continuous Tense. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Past Continuous Tense. The format in the active voice is: Subject + was/were + V1+ing + Object. Thus, the format in Passive voice will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + was/were + being + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case). For example: They were watching a movie. (Active) A movie was being watched by them. (Passive) The options except (d) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is option (d): Ancient manuscripts were being meticulously examined by the historians to piece together the history of the forgotten kingdom from the medieval era. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. FATUOUS
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d) Sagacious. FATUOUS: This means silly, pointless, or complacently foolish. (a) Inane: This means silly or stupid. This is a synonym. (b) Obstinate: This means stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion. This is unrelated. (c) Vacuous: This means having or showing a lack of thought or intelligence; mindless. This is also a synonym. (d) Sagacious: This means having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment; wise or shrewd. This is the direct antonym of fatuous.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: 'Soveriegn' is the incorrectly spelt word. The correct spelling is 'sovereign' and it means 'a supreme ruler, especially a monarch'. Hence, the correct answer is option (c). Stoicism - the quality of experiencing pain or trouble without complaining or showing your emotions

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The family _________ their residence tomorrow.
questions.explanation: The blank requires a verb that denotes an action in the future tense as it can be noted that the event is expected to happen 'tomorrow'. 'Was shifted' and 'were shifted' denote past tense and 'are shifted' denotes present tense and so they are negated. 'Will shift' is the most appropriate option that denotes the action in future tense and also matches the context of the sentence. Hence, the correct answer is option (c). The correct sentence is: The family will shift their residence tomorrow. Note: Tense: Grammatical tense tells us or expresses the time period that the action happens in. It can be happening now (present) or has happened already (past) or will happen later (future). Tenses help us understand how to use verbs correctly to indicate when the action took place.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate phrasal verb. The librarian asked the students to ____ their identity cards before entering the reading room.
questions.explanation: Solution: Hand over means to give something to someone in authority or for checking. Since students are required to give their identity cards to the librarian, hand over fits the context. Hand in means submit officially, hand out means distribute, and hand off means pass responsibility. Complete sentence: The librarian asked the students to hand over their identity cards before entering the reading room. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct semantic fit: The professor criticised the paper for its excessive ___, arguing that clarity had been sacrificed for display.
questions.explanation: Ostentation refers to showy or pretentious display, which fits the idea of prioritising display over clarity. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. Originality - denotes novelty or creativity; opposite of the criticism implied. Objectivity - means impartiality and factual accuracy, not related to excessive display. Orthodoxy - refers to adherence to traditional or established beliefs, not stylistic showiness.

questions.question: Select the sentence containing the homonym of the highlighted word: The detective pursued his quarry through the city's dark alleys.
options: The lioness patiently stalked her quarry across the plains.
After a long investigation, the journalist finally cornered her quarry.
The falcon is a natural predator, swift in catching its quarry.
A large quarry was excavated on the edge of town to mine for limestone.
questions.explanation: A homonym is a word that is spelled and pronounced the same as another word but has a different meaning. In the original sentence, "quarry" refers to a person or animal being hunted or pursued. In options (a), (b), and (c), "quarry" is used with the exact same meaning: the prey or target being stalked or pursued. In option (d), "quarry" refers to a large, deep pit from which stone or other materials are extracted. This is a completely different meaning, making it the correct homonym. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Due to the obnoxious weather, the poultry was dropping like flies and the farmer suffered a huge loss.
questions.explanation: ·"Poultry" is a word that is singular in form but plural in meaning. As such words are plural in meaning, they take a plural verb. ·For example: The poultry were moved from one village to the other. ·The use of "poultries" is incorrect because no such word exists. Thus, option (b) and option (c) are incorrect. ·The use of the verb "was" is also incorrect because 'poultry" agrees with a plural verb. Thus, improvement is required. ·Thus, "poultry were" should replace "poultry was". ·Therefore, the correct answer is: (a) poultry were. ·The corrected form of the sentence is: Due to the obnoxious weather, the poultry were dropping like flies and the farmer suffered a huge loss. Additional Information: ·Words that are singular in form but plural in meaning. As they are plural in meaning, they take a plural verb. Cattle Peasantry Folk Poultry Police Infantry Children

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error: The committee recommended that the policy draft (1)/ be revised thoroughly before publication (2)/ so that potential loopholes can be identified (3)/ and readers may not misunderstand its intended implications. (4)
options: so that potential loopholes can be identified (3)
The committee recommended that the policy draft (1)
and readers may not misunderstand its intended implications (4)
questions.explanation: Part (4) incorrectly uses the modal "may not" to express prevention. In purpose clauses with "so that," when the intended meaning is to prevent something from happening, the correct construction is "do not" / "does not" / "will not" depending on tense, not "may not." "May not" expresses the possibility of lack of permission or the possibility of non-occurrence, not prevention. Correct: "..and readers do not misunderstand its intended implications." Hence, the error is in part (4). Note: In purpose clauses ("so that"), use: Can/cannot (present ability) will/will not (future result) do/does + not (general prevention) Avoid using may not when expressing prevention.

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error: Because neither of the two proposals (1)/ addresses the long-standing infrastructure issues adequately, (2)/ the board decided to postpone the final approval (3)/ until further recommendations are not submitted. (4)
options: Because neither of the two proposals (1)
addresses the long-standing infrastructure issues adequately, (2)
until further recommendations are not submitted (4)
the board decided to postpone the final approval (3)
questions.explanation: Part (4) uses an incorrect negative construction. "Until further recommendations are not submitted" creates a double-negative meaning. The sentence intends to convey postponement until recommendations are submitted. Correct: "..until further recommendations are submitted." Hence, the error is in part (4). Note: In negative constructions with "until," we avoid double negatives (e.g., until X is not).

questions.question: Choose the correct antonym of "REBARBATIVE".
questions.explanation: "Rebarbative" means repellent or unattractive. (a) Abhorrent = disgusting (b) Repellant = causing aversion (c) Attractive = pleasing (d) Off-putting = unpleasant Only (c) expresses the opposite meaning. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. True leadership is not merely about holding a position of power but rather about inspiring and motivating others to achieve shared goals, fostering a sense of purpose and collective responsibility. B. Great leaders demonstrate empathy, resilience, and adaptability, recognizing that success comes from collaboration and the ability to guide others through times of uncertainty and change. C. They lead by example, showing integrity and commitment, while empowering their teams to take ownership of their roles, fostering innovation and a strong sense of loyalty within the organization. D. Leadership is an evolving process, requiring continual self-reflection and growth, as leaders strive to meet the changing demands of their roles and the needs of the people they serve.
questions.explanation: • The sentences talk about 'leadership'. If they are read carefully, it can be noted that A, B and D are independent sentences, which have the quality to be the first sentence in the order. However, it is sentence D that comes as the first sentence, as it introduces the main topic of discussion in the remaining sentences. • It is followed by the pair BC, which talks about great leaders, the qualities possessed by them, and how to foster growth and innovation in their respective roles. • Sentence A concludes the discussion by stating what true leadership is. So, the final order is DBCA. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts - P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. 1: Renewable energy is increasingly seen as a solution to climate change. P. These sources generate power without emitting greenhouse gases. Q. Fossil fuels contribute significantly to global warming. R. Wind, solar, and hydro energy are sustainable alternatives. S. Reducing dependence on fossil fuels lowers carbon emissions. 6: Transitioning to renewables is therefore essential for environmental sustainability.
questions.explanation: After the introduction in (1), (Q) explains the problem with fossil fuels. (S) follows with the benefit of reducing their use. (R) introduces renewable alternatives. (P) explains why these alternatives are beneficial, leading to the conclusion. (6) is a concluding statement. Thus, the correct sequence is QSRP. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct indirect speech form of the following sentence: The project manager said, "My team has completed the crucial first phase of the project, and we have already submitted the final report for your review."
options: The project manager said that his team completed the crucial first phase of the project, and they had already submitted the final report for my review.
The project manager said that his team has completed the crucial first phase of the project, and they have already submitted the final report for my review.
The project manager said that his team had completed the crucial first phase of the project, and they had already submitted the final report for my review.
The project manager said that his team had been completing the crucial first phase of the project, and they had already submitted the final report for my review.
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Direct Speech is in Present Perfect Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Indirect Speech should be in Past Perfect Tense. Present Perfect (Subject + has/have + V3 +Object) should be converted into Past Perfect (Subject + had + V3 +Object). For example: He said, "I have got a new job." (Direct Speech) He said that he had got a new job. (Indirect Speech) The following things in the sentence should change when changed from Direct speech to Indirect Speech: · My changes to his. · has completed changes to had completed. · we changes to they. · have already submitted changes to had already submitted. · your changes to my Therefore, the correct answer is: "The project manager said that his team had completed the crucial first phase of the project, and they had already submitted the final report for my review." Hence, option (c) is correct. Note: V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the phrase. Take French leave.
options: Make an unannounced or unauthorised departure
questions.explanation: The phrase 'take French leave' means to make an unannounced or unauthorised departure. Usage - Early in the wedding reception I had to take French leave to address a family crisis. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: VITUPERATE
questions.explanation: Vituperate - to blame or insult (someone) in strong or violent language; to revile. Berate - to scold or criticize (someone) angrily. Both words describe the act of scolding or criticizing someone harshly and angrily. Hence, option c) is the correct answer. Commend - to praise formally or officially. Operate - to function in a particular way; to be in effect. Cooperate - to work jointly toward the same end.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The beauty of the sunset was ineffable, leaving everyone speechless with its sublime colours.
questions.explanation: "Ineffable" means something that is too great or beautiful to be expressed or described in words. Unforgettable: Something that cannot be forgotten; memorable. Indescribable: Impossible to describe adequately in words; beyond description. Explicable: Capable of being explained or understood. Insufferable: Intolerable or unbearable; difficult to endure. The most appropriate synonym from the given options is "indescribable," which means impossible to adequately describe in words. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Spot the correct spelling of the repetition of a word or phrase at the beginning of successive clauses.
questions.explanation: Anaphora is repetition of a word or expression at the beginning of successive phrases, clauses, sentences, or verses, especially for rhetorical or poetic effect Lincoln's "we cannot dedicate - we cannot consecrate- we cannot hallow- this ground" is an example of anaphora. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the phrase. Lay something up in lavender.
options: Preserve something carefully for future use.
Utilise something expensive on a current happy occasion.
Be obsessed with the lavender produce.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'lay something up in lavender' means to preserve something carefully for future use. Usage - The great house was sold off, and the possessions were laid up in lavender to await auction. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.
